title: HIV in Canada - People living with HIV and new HIV infections, 2020 - Canada.ca
content:
## HIV in Canada - People living with HIV and new HIV infections, 2020
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 420 KB, 1 page)
Organization: Public Health Agency of Canada
At the end of 2020, an estimated 62,790 people were living with HIV (PLHIV) in Canada
Map of Canada with provincial/regional estimates of PLHIV
Among the PLHIV, approximately:
- 1 in 2 were gay, bisexual and other men who have sex with men (gbMSM)
- 1 in 3 were heterosexual
- 1 in 8 were people who inject drugs (PWID) Footnote 1
- 1 in 10 were Indigenous peoples
- In 2020, an estimated 1,520 new HIV infections occurred.
- This means 4 people were infected with HIV in Canada everyday
Compared to 2018, the number of new HIV infections in 2020 has
- Increased among PWID (3.4%)
- Decreased among gbMSM (18.7%)
- Decreased among Heterosexual people (15.7%)
- Decreased among gbMSM-PWID (4.4%)
- Decreased among Females (8.3%)
- Decreased among Males (15.9%)
The occurrence of new HIV infections is higher among some key populations
The estimated 2020 HIV incidence rates (per 100,000 population) for key populations and Canada overall were:
- 239.4 among people who have injected drugs in the past 6-12 months
- 166.2 sexually active gbMSM Footnote 2
- 15.2 Indigenous peoples
- 4.0 overall Canadian population
PWID includes people with a history of injection drugs use
Return to footnote 1 referrer
Sexually active  gbMSM defined as: gay, bisexual-identifing AND non-gay/bisexual-identifing men  who have sex with men reporting anal sex with a man in the past 6-12 months.  Sorge J, Colyer S, Cox J, Kroch A, Lachowsky N, Popovic, N, Yang Q. Estimation  of the Population Size of Gay, Bisexual and Other Men Who Have Sex with Men in  Canada, 2020. International AIDS Conference, 2022
